data.title: Xcel Brands prices public offering at $1.10 per share, totalling $2.6 million with 2,181,818 shares. CEO buys 145,147 shares at $1.36 each in private placement. Closing on/about Aug 4, 2025.
data.description: Xcel Brands revealed today the pricing details of its recent public offering, consisting of 2,181,818 shares of common stock priced at $1.10 each. Additionally, in a private placement, the company will issue 145,147 unregistered shares to insiders, including CEO Robert D'Loren, at $1.36 per share, mirroring the closing price on July 31, 2025. The completion of the offering is scheduled for around August 4, 2025, pending the fulfillment of standard closing requirements.

data.title: "The HueHub in Miami aims to impact traditional housing with 7 towers, 4,032 units, spanning 509,447 sq ft on 12 acres."
data.description: In Miami, The HueHub is addressing the pressing need for affordable housing by introducing a unique concept catered towards the missing middle class. This groundbreaking mixed-use development spans across roughly 12 acres, totaling 509,447 square feet. The project includes seven impressive 35-story towers, offering a total of 4,032 carefully planned and fully furnished residential units. The HueHub aims to provide a transformative solution to Miami's housing crisis.

data.title: InterTalk chosen to deploy Enlite Cloud Dispatch system at Quad Cities International Airport in Moline, Illinois.
data.description: In Dartmouth, NS, on August 1, 2025, InterTalk™ Critical Information Systems, a top provider of mission-critical dispatch systems, has been chosen to implement its Enlite™ Cloud Dispatch system at Quad Cities International Airport in Moline, Illinois. This move represents a significant advancement for the airport in improving its operational efficiency and emergency response capabilities.

data.title: Growth in fraud detection and prevention market due to increasing digital transactions and companies' digital transformation initiatives.
data.description: A report titled "Fraud Detection and Prevention (Know Your User), 2025: A Frost RadarT Report" is now available through ResearchAndMarkets.com. The market for fraud detection and prevention (FDP) has seen substantial growth due to the rise in frequency, sophistication, and volume of fraudulent attacks. With the surge in digital transactions and companies' digital transformation efforts, there is a larger attack surface. Businesses are increasingly incorporating FDP solutions utilizing artificial intelligence.

data.title: Moderna Inc. reported Q2 loss of $2.13 per share, beating consensus of $2.98 loss, with quarterly sales of $142 million, surpassing consensus of $116.34 million. Sales declined by 41% from last year, mostly due to lower COVID-19 vaccine sales at $114 million for the quarter. The company anticipates demand to concentrate in second half of the year as the vaccine transitions to seasonal product. Moderna reported $114 million in Spikevax sales in Q2, including $88 million in U.S. and $26 million in international sales. FDA approved Moderna's Spikevax for children 6 months to 11 years of age at increased risk for COVID-19, and the company also received final approval for Spikevax from European Medicines Agency targeting LP.8.1 variant for individuals six months and older. FDA also approved mNEXSPIKE, a next-generation COVID-19 vaccine for all adults aged 65 and older.
data.description: Moderna Inc. reported a second-quarter loss of $2.13 per share, surpassing expectations of a $2.98 loss and an improvement from a loss of $3.33 a year ago. The COVID-19 vaccine maker recorded quarterly sales of $142 million, exceeding the consensus of $116.34 million, with a 41% decrease from $241 million in the same period last year. This decline was mainly due to lower COVID-19 vaccine sales of $114 million in the quarter. Demand is anticipated to rise in the second half of the year as the vaccine becomes a seasonal respiratory product. In the second quarter of 2025, Moderna recorded $114 million in Spikevax sales, including $88 million in the U.S. and $26 million internationally. The FDA approved the company's supplemental Biologics License Application for Spikevax in children aged 6 months to 11 years at risk for COVID-19. Additionally, FDA gave the green light for mNEXSPIKE, Moderna's next-generation COVID-19 vaccine, for use in all adults aged 65 and above. The European Medicines Agency approved Spikevax for LP.8.1 variant targeting in individuals aged six months and above.

data.title: ACR Poker introduces Daily Mystery Bounty events with 10% top prize pool bounty starting August 3rd.
data.description: A lucrative highest bounty of 10% of the entire prize pool will be available this weekend in SAN JOSE, Costa Rica. ACR Poker revealed the introduction of Daily Mystery Bounty competitions in its tournament schedule, commencing this Sunday on August 3rd. This promises an unparalleled opportunity for participants to win big.

data.title: HabitNu chosen by CMS for National Health Tech Ecosystem, focusing on diabetes and obesity prevention.Characters: 101
data.description: A digital health company known as HabitNu, acknowledged by the CDC for its focus on promoting lifestyle changes for diabetes, obesity, and chronic disease prevention, was recently chosen by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) to be part of the National Health Tech Ecosystem. The recognition highlights the company's commitment to improving public health by addressing prevalent health issues. This decision signifies an important step forward in the company's mission to impact healthcare in a positive way.

data.title: Collier County Sheriff's Office now equipped every patrol car with LifeVac devices thanks to Cassata Foundation donation.
data.description: In Naples, Florida, on August 1, 2025, it was announced that all patrol cars in the Collier County Sheriff's Office are now outfitted with LifeVac choking rescue devices. This move marks a crucial advancement in first responder safety and public health. The initiative was facilitated by a noteworthy donation, ensuring that each patrol car, totaling X units, now carries this life-saving equipment.
